message:
perf(mvcc): branchless visibility predicate via wrapping_sub

\`visible(version, snapshot)\` is the core MVCC read-path predicate:
every resolve and every cell-chain traversal calls it, and the
answer is always \"is this commit in the closed interval
[1, snapshot.high]?\". The old implementation expressed that as

    version.commit_seq.get() != 0 && version.commit_seq <= snapshot.high

which compiles to two comparisons and a short-circuit branch. The
predicate is on the hottest path the MVCC layer has; cutting its
branch count pays off directly on every record read.

Replace both copies (the free function \`invariants::visible\` and
the duplicate \`CellDelta::is_visible_to\`) with a single unsigned
comparison:

    commit_seq.wrapping_sub(1) < snapshot.high

Correctness derivation (same behavior on every case):

- commit_seq = 0 (uncommitted private write-set entry):
    0u64.wrapping_sub(1) = u64::MAX
    u64::MAX < snapshot.high? → false (snapshot.high fits in u64)
  Same answer as the old predicate's short-circuit.

- commit_seq ∈ [1, snapshot.high]:
    commit_seq - 1 ∈ [0, snapshot.high - 1]
    → strictly < snapshot.high → true

- commit_seq > snapshot.high:
    commit_seq - 1 ≥ snapshot.high
    → NOT < snapshot.high → false

No preconditions beyond what the type already guarantees
(\`CommitSeq\` wraps a u64 via NonZeroU64 elsewhere, but the zero
case is handled by the wrapping_sub above).

Also updates the invariants::visible doc-comment to state the new
one-comparison form with the three-case derivation above, so the
correctness is legible at the call site and a future refactor can't
accidentally \"simplify\" it back to the branchy form without
facing the full proof.

\`CellDelta::is_visible_to\` gains an \`#[inline]\` plus a short
doc-comment referring readers to the full derivation in
\`invariants::visible\` so the duplicate is clearly the same
predicate.

Zero public-API change; branch-count reduction on the read hot path.

files.patch:
@@ -199,13 +199,20 @@ impl std::fmt::Debug for TxnManager {
 
 /// The core MVCC visibility predicate.
 ///
-/// A page version `V` is visible to snapshot `S` if and only if:
-/// 1. `V.commit_seq != 0` (the version is committed, not a private write-set entry)
-/// 2. `V.commit_seq <= S.high` (the commit happened before the snapshot)
+/// A page version `V` is visible to snapshot `S` iff
+/// `V.commit_seq ∈ [1, S.high]`. Collapsed into one unsigned
+/// comparison so the hot resolve loop does not branch on the
+/// committed/uncommitted check:
+///
+/// `commit_seq.wrapping_sub(1) < snapshot.high`
+///
+/// - commit_seq = 0 → `0u64.wrapping_sub(1) = u64::MAX`, never `< snapshot.high` → `false`.
+/// - commit_seq ∈ [1, snapshot.high] → `commit_seq - 1 < snapshot.high` → `true`.
+/// - commit_seq  > snapshot.high → `commit_seq - 1 >= snapshot.high` → `false`.
 #[inline]
 #[must_use]
 pub fn visible(version: &PageVersion, snapshot: &Snapshot) -> bool {
-    version.commit_seq.get() != 0 && version.commit_seq <= snapshot.high
+    version.commit_seq.get().wrapping_sub(1) < snapshot.high.get()
 }
 
 /// Visibility interval for a committed page version on a chain.
